summary: Compares approaches to electoral fraud types across systems, analyzing ballot stuffing, voter intimidation, vote buying, gerrymandering.
key_points: Different countries adopt varying approaches to electoral fraud types.
Comparative analysis reveals: ballot stuffing, voter intimidation, vote buying, gerrymandering.
Institutional context shapes how this factor operates in practice.
Reform efforts draw on lessons from other jurisdictions.
No single model dominates; contextual fit matters.
